experiment.title: Phase 81 Non-Determinism Impact Measurement
experiment.description: 10x per-domain repeated measurement separating fixed-input response variance from round-metric variance.
experiment.methodology: Fixed-input repetitions plus repeated full round measurements against the Phase 80 baseline path.

claims.text: hallucination showed measured validator-facing variance across repeated local runs; miners remain adversarial, determinism is best-effort only, validators cannot force exact miner inference behavior, and future controls must remain backward-compatible and commodity-hardware feasible.
observed_variance: hallucination fixed-input mean score std=0.0000 with 0 fixed-input threshold flips.
inferred_sources: The repeated score spread may reflect nondeterministic GPU kernel execution or runtime-level scheduling effects in the local stack.
The response diversity may reflect request batching or scheduling sensitivity even when validator-controlled inputs are held constant.
Near-threshold movement may reflect floating-point accumulation order or kernel-level numeric drift rather than a semantic behavior change.
This source is validator-controlled harness randomness, not miner behavior, and must stay separated from runtime variance when interpreting results.
recommended_mitigations: Treat single-run scores as provisional and size future validator thresholds against repeated-run spread before tightening penalties.
Benchmark threshold choices on a stable local load profile and compare spread before and after serving-stack changes.
Prefer margin-aware or repeated-run validator comparisons in later phases instead of treating exact threshold crossings as fully stable.
Keep the Phase 81 measurement path fixed-input only, and preserve the existing seed-increment baseline path for comparability without conflating the two.

gpu_kernel.observed_signal: hallucination fixed-input reward spread std=0.0000 with 0 threshold flips.
gpu_kernel.inference: The repeated score spread may reflect nondeterministic GPU kernel execution or runtime-level scheduling effects in the local stack.
gpu_kernel.mitigation: Treat single-run scores as provisional and size future validator thresholds against repeated-run spread before tightening penalties.

batching_scheduling.observed_signal: hallucination produced 1 unique repeated responses under a fixed seed and fixed sample IDs.
batching_scheduling.inference: The response diversity may reflect request batching or scheduling sensitivity even when validator-controlled inputs are held constant.
batching_scheduling.mitigation: Benchmark threshold choices on a stable local load profile and compare spread before and after serving-stack changes.

floating_point_kernel.observed_signal: hallucination response-score band ranged from 0.3451 to 0.3451 around DECISION_THRESHOLD=0.5.
floating_point_kernel.inference: Near-threshold movement may reflect floating-point accumulation order or kernel-level numeric drift rather than a semantic behavior change.
floating_point_kernel.mitigation: Prefer margin-aware or repeated-run validator comparisons in later phases instead of treating exact threshold crossings as fully stable.

harness_level_randomness.observed_signal: hallucination fixed-input repeats reused sample_ids=['eval-hall-164', 'eval-hall-029', 'eval-hall-007', 'eval-hall-190', 'eval-hall-071', 'eval-hall-063', 'eval-hall-058', 'eval-hall-036', 'eval-hall-189', 'eval-hall-027', 'eval-hall-174', 'eval-hall-140', 'eval-hall-023', 'eval-hall-152', 'eval-hall-109', 'eval-hall-009', 'eval-hall-008', 'eval-hall-024', 'eval-hall-056', 'eval-hall-060'] and seed=42 while the baseline runner still increments round seeds.
harness_level_randomness.inference: This source is validator-controlled harness randomness, not miner behavior, and must stay separated from runtime variance when interpreting results.
harness_level_randomness.mitigation: Keep the Phase 81 measurement path fixed-input only, and preserve the existing seed-increment baseline path for comparability without conflating the two.

citations.citation_context: Observed repeated-run variance motivates measuring spread, not single-point scores.
Runtime reproducibility remains scoped to environment and scheduling controls.
Floating-point, batching, and hardware execution order remain plausible sources of variance.
